question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $68,000 on April 20?

fragility_points: Short-horizon crypto volatility could erase the cushion quickly.
Minute-specific settlement mechanics create concentrated timing risk.
The view depends on current regime persistence more than on a fresh bullish catalyst.
key_assumptions: BTC stays in roughly its current trading regime through April 20.
No macro or crypto-specific shock forces Binance BTC/USDT below 68000 by the settlement minute.
Binance remains a usable and representative resolution source without a material anomaly in the relevant 1-minute candle.
main_logical_chain: The contract settles on Binance BTC/USDT at the 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close on April 20.
Current Binance spot and recent Binance closes place BTC materially above 68000.
For a five-day threshold market, a level this far below current spot usually resolves Yes unless a material shock intervenes.
Because the settlement is exchange- and minute-specific, residual tail risk remains larger than the market's tiny implied No probability.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is already comfortably above 68000 on Binance and recent realized prices support a high Yes base rate, though short-horizon volatility and exact minute-specific settlement risk keep the estimate below the market.
quote_anchors: The governing source of truth is Binance BTC/USDT, specifically the 1-minute candle for 12:00 ET on April 20, 2026.
My outside-view estimate is 94% rather than the market-implied ~97.15%.
payload.source_quality_view: High-quality direct contract and exchange sources were used, plus one independent contextual price check; independence is medium because Binance is both evidence source and settlement venue.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can move several thousand dollars in a few days, and this contract resolves on one exact minute on one exchange.
A Binance-specific wick or operational anomaly at noon ET could still produce No.
strongest_supports: Binance spot during research was around 74.6k, leaving about a 6.5k cushion above the threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes from April 7 through April 15 were all above 68000.
CoinGecko provided an independent contextual check showing BTC broadly trading in the same regime across venues.
payload.timing_relevance: This is a five-day, date-sensitive market with settlement tied to the April 20 noon ET candle, so current distance from the threshold and near-term volatility matter much more than long-run BTC fundamentals.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much probability to assign to a sharp drawdown over the next five days.
Operational ambiguity around exact final-candle handling if Binance displays or serves data inconsistently.
payload.what_would_change_view: A drop back toward the high-60k range, a new macro or liquidation shock, or evidence of Binance-specific instability around the noon ET settlement process would lower the Yes estimate materially.

fragility_points: Weekend geopolitical or macro shock could quickly compress the cushion.
Liquidation-style downside acceleration back toward 70k would undermine the thesis fast.
Binance-specific operational issues could matter disproportionately in this contract.
key_assumptions: No major unscheduled macro or crypto-specific shock hits before noon ET on April 20.
Binance remains a usable settlement source without odd operational distortion at the relevant minute.
Recent BTC volatility does not erase a roughly 9% cushion absent a genuine new downside catalyst.
main_logical_chain: The market implies about 97.15% that Binance BTC/USDT will close above 68000 at noon ET on April 20.
Current BTCUSDT spot is around 74.9k, so failure requires about a 9% drop by the exact settlement minute.
Verified scheduled macro catalysts checked in this run occur after April 20, reducing the odds of a known calendar-driven repricing before settlement.
Because unscheduled shock risk and exact-minute settlement fragility still matter, the probability should be high but below the market's near-certainty.
payload.main_thesis: BTC is likely to settle above 68000 on April 20 because spot is near 74.9k, the threshold is about 9% lower, and no obvious scheduled macro catalyst was verified before resolution.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title.
payload.source_quality_view: Primary contract rules and Binance API data were strong direct sources; Fed and BEA calendars were strong official contextual sources; overall source-of-truth ambiguity was low-to-medium because the wording is clear but the one-minute settlement structure is operationally fragile.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC traded as low as 67732.01 in recent daily data, so 68000 is not a remote tail level.
A one-minute exchange-specific settlement print makes extreme confidence vulnerable to an odd sharp selloff or operational distortion at the exact timestamp.
strongest_supports: Binance spot during the run was about 74895.57, leaving roughly a 9.2% cushion above 68000.
Verified official calendars place the next FOMC meeting on April 28-29 and BEA GDP/Personal Income releases on April 30, both after resolution.
The contract only requires the exact settlement minute close to be above 68000, not a sustained multi-day hold.
payload.timing_relevance: The thesis is mainly calendar-driven: no obvious scheduled macro catalyst was verified before resolution, so the key remaining risk is unscheduled shock before the exact noon ET settlement minute.
unresolved_ambiguities: No complete high-frequency positioning or ETF-flow dataset was available in this run.
Unscheduled catalysts remain inherently hard to bound over the remaining window.
payload.what_would_change_view: A decisive break of the low-70k area, a major risk-off shock, or Binance-specific settlement concerns would move the estimate materially lower.

fragility_points: A sharp multi-day BTC selloff would erode the cushion quickly.
Exact-minute settlement increases path/timing sensitivity versus a broader close definition.
Evidence is strong for current level, weaker for several-day-forward stability.
key_assumptions: BTC remains in roughly the current mid-74k trading regime through the resolution window.
No Binance-specific price dislocation or operational issue distorts the April 20 12:00 ET close.
A roughly 8.9% cushion to strike is large enough to make Yes highly likely but not near-certain.
main_logical_chain: The market implies 97.15% Yes and current Binance BTCUSDT is around 74.6k, leaving a substantial cushion above 68k.
Because the contract is defined by a single Binance 1-minute close at 12:00 ET on April 20, residual downside and venue-specific risk still matter.
That makes Yes strongly favored, but a modest discount to market extremity is warranted, leading to a 93% estimate.
payload.main_thesis: The market is directionally right that BTC > 68k on April 20 is very likely, but 97.15% looks slightly overconfident given several days of crypto volatility and exact-minute Binance settlement risk.

strongest_disconfirmers: Contract resolves on one exact future one-minute close, not current spot.

payload.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves at the Binance BTCUSDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-20, so date, timezone, and exact minute materially matter.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much short-horizon volatility traders should price into a single-minute settlement several days out.

payload.what_would_change_view: A sharp BTC drawdown toward or below 68k, rising Binance-vs-broader-spot dispersion, or credible evidence of Binance-specific settlement fragility would lower the estimate materially.

quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.

payload.what_would_change_view: I would move toward the market if BTC stays firmly above low-70k with clean Binance data into April 19-20; I would move away if BTC compresses toward 68k, if volatility spikes, or if Binance shows operational or pricing anomalies.
